device_context:
Device Name: Continuous Glucose Monitor
Device Class: Class II
Risk Profile: Moderate
Intended User: diabetes self-management patients aged 14+
Use Environment: patient home
Indication for Use: Continuous interstitial glucose measurement for patients with diabetes mellitus to support insulin dosing decisions.
instruction: Draft a Design Inputs SOP per ISO 13485 §7.3.3 and 21 CFR 820.30(c) for the device below.
target:
# SOP-DI-001: Design Inputs — Continuous Glucose Monitor

## 1. Purpose
Defines the procedure for capturing, reviewing, and approving design inputs for the Continuous Glucose Monitor in compliance with ISO 13485:2016 §7.3.3 and 21 CFR 820.30(c).

## 2. Scope
Applies to all design input activities for the CGM (Class II, moderate risk) used by patients self-managing diabetes.

## 3. Procedure
### 3.1 Capture (ISO 13485 §7.3.3(a)–(e); 21 CFR 820.30(c))
Design inputs shall include: functional, performance, usability, and safety requirements; applicable statutory and regulatory requirements; outputs of risk management per ISO 14971; and where applicable, information from previous similar designs.

### 3.2 Review and Approval
Inputs shall be reviewed for adequacy, approved, and shall not contain incomplete, ambiguous, or conflicting requirements.

## 4. References
- ISO 13485:2016 §7.3.3
- 21 CFR 820.30(c)
- IEC 62366-1 (usability)
- ISO 14971:2019

device_context:
Device Name: Implantable Cardiac Pacemaker
Device Class: Class III
Risk Profile: High
Intended User: interventional cardiologists
Use Environment: cath lab
Indication for Use: Treatment of symptomatic bradyarrhythmias.
instruction: Draft a Design Validation SOP per ISO 13485 §7.3.7 for the device below.
target:
# SOP-DV-001: Design Validation — Implantable Cardiac Pacemaker

## 1. Purpose
Defines design validation activities for the Implantable Cardiac Pacemaker per ISO 13485:2016 §7.3.7 and 21 CFR 820.30(g).

## 2. Procedure
### 2.1 Validation Plan
Validation shall be performed under defined operating conditions on initial production units, equivalents thereof, or simulated use environments. Given the Class III, high-risk profile, clinical evaluation per MDR Annex XIV is mandatory.

### 2.2 Records
Validation records shall identify the design, method(s), date, and individual(s) performing the validation, and shall be retained in the Design History File (21 CFR 820.30(j)).

## 3. References
- ISO 13485:2016 §7.3.7
- 21 CFR 820.30(g), (j)
- ISO 14155 (clinical investigations)
